title: Rushup Edge Mk.I Memorial
location_name: Rushup Edge, Peak District (Derbyshire / South Pennines)
crew_summary: All killed (flew into southern side of Rushup Edge)

external_narratives.summary: Detailed crash investigation page for Hampden X3154 of No. 106 Squadron RAF, which crashed into the southern side of Rushup Edge on 21 December 1940 during a night navigation exercise from RAF Finningley. The aircraft was 50 miles off track at the time of impact.
key_details: Inquiry found pilot and navigator responsible

external_narratives.summary: Crash site description with details of the crew: P/O Michael Hubbard (pilot, aged 20), Sgt Kenneth W.B. Perkins (navigator, aged 24), Sgt Derrick J. Davey (W/Op/Air Gunner, aged 18), and Sgt David W. Smith (Air Gunner, aged 19). Very little wreckage remains at the upland pasture site.
key_details: Site on upland pasture near road

external_narratives.summary: Casualty record for Pilot Officer Michael Hubbard, pilot of Hampden X3154. Provides service details and commemoration information for this 20-year-old officer killed in the crash.
key_details: P/O Michael Hubbard aged 20 at death

external_narratives.summary: Aviation accident database entry for Hampden X3154 crash on 21 December 1940. Records the aircraft flying into the hillside during a night cross-country navigation exercise.
